notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix -> /nw-design -> /nw-deliver. The validated RCA at deliver/rca.md (Option B chosen) and the PINNED surface spec at design/architecture.md §3-§7 are the authoritative specification. The ADR-0063 rev-8 amendment (Status changelog entry + D6 rev-8 block) is ALREADY APPLIED to docs/product/architecture/adr-0063-built-in-ca-port-trait-and-root-key-protection.md by the DESIGN-side dispatch that produced this roadmap.

SINGLE cohesive step (01-01). Per RCA §5 Option B and architecture.md §3, this fix is one fully-pinned unit: one additive `ObservationStore` trait method + two adapter impls (host redb counter table + sim Mutex counter) + one call-site swap in `ca_issuance.rs` + rustdoc updates + one DST conformance test. It CANNOT be meaningfully split into separate compiling steps: adding the trait method without BOTH adapter impls fails to compile (the trait has two production implementors — `LocalObservationStore` and `SimObservationStore` — both of which must implement any new non-defaulted method). A RED-then-GREEN split across commits is NOT applicable here in the usual sense — see the next note on why a classic red-regression test is not constructible.

Why this is NOT a classic red-regression bugfix (RCA §4). The retired precondition IS 'do not call `issue_and_audit` concurrently for one store'. A regression test that makes today's `len()`-derived ordinal collide must call the seam concurrently — i.e. it must VIOLATE precondition 1 by construction. There is no in-contract input that produces the collision against the serialized HEAD code, so a red-against-current-behaviour regression test is NOT constructible. The correct framing (RCA §4, architecture.md §4.3): the DST equivalence conformance test pins the FIX's contract — monotonic-and-unique-under-concurrency, independence-from-the-audit-table (not-derived-from-len), and host-durable-across-reopen. The conformance harness IS the primary test deliverable; it goes green when the fix lands and would have been impossible to satisfy with the `len()` derivation.

Pinned surface (architecture.md §3.1, wave-decisions.md 'Pinned signature'). The ONE additive method is `async fn next_issuance_ordinal(&self) -> Result<IssuanceOrdinal, ObservationStoreError>;` on `trait ObservationStore`. It returns the `overdrive_core::id::IssuanceOrdinal` newtype (NOT a bare u64 — D2; the newtype is already imported in the trait module). No other `ObservationStore` method signature changes. No new `CaIssuanceError` variant (D9 — the RCA Option-A `OrdinalCollision` detection variant is explicitly NOT part of this fix; there is no collision to detect once it is unrepresentable). No row / envelope / golden-bytes / append-only-serial-guard change (the ordinal stays a caller-set field on the V1 payload; only its SOURCE changes).

Fresh-store + gap + saturation semantics (architecture.md §3.1 'Edge cases', D3/D4). Fresh store: the first call returns `IssuanceOrdinal::new(0)` (matches pre-fix `len()`-of-empty-table semantics; keeps existing 'ordinals start at 0' consumer expectation and the V1 golden fixtures valid). Gap semantics (D4): the counter advance is committed unconditionally on Ok; an allocated-but-unwritten ordinal (mint/audit-write fails after allocation) is BURNED — the sequence may have holes. This is CORRECT: the consumer projection `max_by_key(issuance_ordinal)` needs strict ORDERING, not density. Callers MUST NOT attempt to reclaim a burned ordinal. Saturation: u64 does not realistically saturate; adapters do NOT special-case overflow (no wrapping/saturating add).

No migration code (D12, architecture.md §8). Greenfield single-cut per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md`: a fresh store starts the counter at 0; 'delete the redb file' is the upgrade path. There is NO 'reconstruct the counter from `len()` of existing rows' path — that would re-introduce the exact `len()`-shaped derivation this fix removes. A pre-rev-8 store is discarded, not migrated.

#36 (multi-node gossip) is OUT OF SCOPE and UNCHANGED (D13, architecture.md §8). Today's `len()` and the new counter are equally node-local; this fix neither improves nor regresses cross-node ordinal uniqueness. The contract is explicitly per-store (= per-node today). Do NOT expand into #36.

#226 disposition (D11, architecture.md §6). The fix CLOSES #226's technical content (the durable counter is the exact mechanism #226 prescribed; the ordinal now survives row deletion because it is no longer a function of `len()`). The orchestrator/user actions #226 close-when-lands; THIS roadmap and the crafter file/edit/close NOTHING on GitHub (CLAUDE.md § 'Never create GH issues without user approval'; incident #161).

CRAFTER CONSTRAINT (CLAUDE.md § 'Implement to the design — never invent API surface'). The signature in design/architecture.md §3.1 / wave-decisions.md 'Pinned signature' is THE contract. Do NOT invent additional public surface: no new error variant, no second trait method, no row-field change, no `Option<IssuanceOrdinal>` sentinel, no 'row-without-ordinal' input type (those were the rejected B2 shape — architecture.md §2). If a primitive seems missing or under-specified, STOP and surface a blocker — do not reach for the nearest mechanism that compiles.

Object-oriented paradigm (project CLAUDE.md). Crafter is `@nw-software-crafter`. The new method is a trait method; the host (`LocalObservationStore`) and sim (`SimObservationStore`) are the concrete adapter implementations.

ADR-0063 rev-8 is already applied (no architect dispatch needed mid-DELIVER). Unlike the fix-observation-lww-merge precedent (which paired an ADR revision with its GREEN step via architect dispatch), the ADR amendment for THIS fix was applied during the DESIGN-side prerequisite dispatch that produced this roadmap. The crafter does NOT edit any ADR. The crafter DOES update implementation rustdoc per architecture.md §5 (the `issue_and_audit` `# Preconditions` / `# Errors` block) — that is source code, not an ADR.

Mutation testing scope (post-GREEN, per .claude/rules/testing.md § Mutation testing). Run with `run_in_background: true` (the mutation exception to foreground-only). Diff-scoped per-file: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs` plus the two adapter files. On macOS this is Lima-wrapped (`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants ...`) because `--features integration-tests` participates. Kill-rate gate >=80%. Do NOT run `git checkout -- crates/` after the pass — cargo-mutants restores the tree itself and the destructive-git-ops hook blocks the checkout (user-memory `no_git_checkout_after_mutants`). The trait method body is largely a delegating signature; the load-bearing mutation surface is the two adapter allocate bodies (the `+ 1` increment, the fresh-store `0`/`1` seed, the read-before-write ordering).

reviews.scope: Fix direction (Option B1 — separate atomic allocation via a new `ObservationStore::next_issuance_ordinal()` port method) + the ADR-0063 rev-8 amendment.
reviews.notes: User explicitly approved Option B1 and the ADR amendment ('adr approved'). The pinned signature, the two adapter contracts (host redb single-key counter table; sim in-memory Mutex counter), the DST equivalence conformance requirement, the retired single-writer precondition, the decoupling of ordinal monotonicity from the append-only audit log, and the no-new-error-variant / no-row-change / no-migration-code constraints are all ratified in design/wave-decisions.md (D1-D13). #226 disposition is user-gated (close-when-lands chosen); the orchestrator actions it after the fix lands.

phases.name: Make the duplicate IssuanceOrdinal unrepresentable via a durable atomic counter
phases.description: Single cohesive Option-B1 fix (RCA §5 / architecture.md §3-§5). Replace the `issued_certificate_rows().await?.len()`-derived issuance ordinal with an atomically-allocated value drawn from a durable, strictly-monotonic counter the `ObservationStore` adapter owns. ONE additive trait method `next_issuance_ordinal(&self) -> Result<IssuanceOrdinal, ObservationStoreError>`; host adapter (`LocalObservationStore`) backs it with a single-key redb counter table allocated atomically in one `begin_write`/`commit` under redb serializable isolation on the `spawn_blocking` pool; sim adapter (`SimObservationStore`) backs it with an in-memory `Mutex<u64>` incremented under the existing parking_lot discipline; `ca_issuance.rs::issue_and_audit` swaps the `len()` block for a single `observation.next_issuance_ordinal().await.map_err(CaIssuanceError::audit)?` and updates its `# Preconditions` (retire single-writer; decouple append-only) and `# Errors` (allocation failure now sources `CaIssuanceError::Audit`) rustdoc. The trait method's full behaviour contract (preconditions / postconditions / edge-cases / observable-invariants) lands per development.md § 'Trait definitions specify behavior'. A new `run_issuance_ordinal_conformance` harness (sibling to `run_lww_conformance` in `overdrive-core::testing::observation_store`) drives BOTH adapters through one allocation sequence and asserts monotonic-and-unique-under-concurrency + independence-from-the-audit-table; a host-only `integration-tests`-gated test asserts durable-across-reopen. No row / envelope / golden-bytes / append-only-serial-guard change. No new error variant. No migration code. Single cohesive commit.

steps.name: Atomic durable issuance-ordinal counter — additive ObservationStore port method, host+sim adapters, call-site swap, DST conformance harness
steps.description: Land the Option-B1 fix in one cohesive commit. (1) `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs` — add EXACTLY the pinned additive method to `trait ObservationStore`: `async fn next_issuance_ordinal(&self) -> Result<IssuanceOrdinal, ObservationStoreError>;` returning the `overdrive_core::id::IssuanceOrdinal` newtype (already imported in this module; do NOT return a bare u64). Add the full rustdoc behaviour contract VERBATIM from architecture.md §3.1 — Preconditions (none; safe to call concurrently for one store; requires no serialization / single-writer / append-only invariant), Postconditions (strictly greater than every prior return for this store including across restart; counter durably advanced past `n` BEFORE returning Ok; NO `issued_certificates` row read or written — NOT a function of `issued_certificate_rows().len()`), Edge cases (fresh store returns `IssuanceOrdinal::new(0)`; allocated-but-unused ordinals are BURNED / gap semantics; u64 does not special-case saturation), Observable invariants (monotonic-and-unique across concurrency; durable across restart with the host/sim split spelled out; independent of the audit table — deleting/compacting/GCing `issued_certificates` does NOT rewind the counter), Errors (`ObservationStoreError::Io` on a backing-store failure; on error NO ordinal is allocated and the counter is unchanged — advance is atomic-or-nothing). NO other `ObservationStore` method signature changes. (2) `crates/overdrive-store-local/src/observation_backend.rs` — host adapter. Add a dedicated single-key redb table `const ISSUANCE_ORDINAL_COUNTER_TABLE: TableDefinition<&[u8], u64> = TableDefinition::new("issuance_ordinal_counter");` (name pinned; additive — never alters existing tables); materialize it in `open()` alongside the other tables. Implement `next_issuance_ordinal` as ONE `begin_write` -> read-current -> insert(current+1 / initial) -> `commit`, on the `spawn_blocking` pool (no blocking redb call on a tokio worker thread, per development.md § 'No blocking std::fs inside async fn'). redb's serializable isolation linearises concurrent writers — the read-increment-write is collision-free (the same TOCTOU-safety the existing `apply_alloc_status_lww` / `apply_issued_certificate` read-before-write rely on). On an absent counter entry (fresh store): return `IssuanceOrdinal::new(0)` and persist `1` as the next value (pin the encoding so 'first call returns 0' holds). The counter table is INDEPENDENT of `ISSUED_CERTIFICATES_TABLE` — deleting audit rows does not touch it (#226 delete-survival). (3) `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/observation_store.rs` — sim adapter. Add a `next_issuance_ordinal: Mutex<u64>` field (initialized to 0) to the inner struct, guarded by the same parking_lot::Mutex discipline the sibling indices use. Implement `next_issuance_ordinal` to lock, read-increment-return under the lock (`let mut n = ...lock(); let v = *n; *n += 1; IssuanceOrdinal::new(v)` — shape, not literal). Single critical section, no `.await` held across the lock (development.md § 'Never hold a lock across .await'). This is the determinism contract: under a seeded DST run the allocation order follows the deterministic call order, so the ordinal trajectory is bit-identical across replays. In-memory is the sanctioned sim durability domain (sim store is never reconstructed mid-run). (4) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/ca_issuance.rs` — call-site swap. Replace the `len()`-derivation block (architecture.md cites lines ~200-211) with the pinned shape: `let ordinal = observation.next_issuance_ordinal().await.map_err(CaIssuanceError::audit)?;` plus the explanatory comment from architecture.md §3.2. The row construction is UNCHANGED — `issuance_ordinal: ordinal` still sets the field from the local `ordinal` (now the allocated value). REUSE `CaIssuanceError::audit` (an allocation failure is an audit-path failure — issuance refuses, no unaudited cert escapes); add NO new `CaIssuanceError` variant. Rewrite the `# Preconditions` rustdoc on `issue_and_audit` per architecture.md §5: precondition 1 (single-writer/serialized) RETIRED (state explicitly: 'The former single-writer precondition is retired as of ADR-0063 rev 8 — the ordinal is allocated from a durable atomic counter, so concurrent issuance against one store no longer collides.'); precondition 2 (append-only) DOWNGRADED/DECOUPLED (the append-only contract remains for its OWN reasons — audit immutability, the serial-dedup guard, the ADR-0067 D10 restart-recovery marker — but ordinal correctness no longer depends on it). Update `# Errors`: `CaIssuanceError::Audit` is now sourced by EITHER the ordinal allocation failure OR the audit-row write failure — change the old '(or the pre-write issuance-ordinal count read failed)' wording to '(or the issuance-ordinal allocation failed)'. (5) `crates/overdrive-core/src/testing/observation_store.rs` — add `pub async fn run_issuance_ordinal_conformance<T: ObservationStore + ?Sized>(store: &T)` as a sibling to the existing `run_lww_conformance`, exercising architecture.md §4.3 assertions 1-2 against any adapter: (a) monotonic-and-unique under concurrency — drive N concurrent/harness-interleaved `next_issuance_ordinal()` calls against one store; assert the N returned ordinals are N distinct, strictly-increasing values; (b) independent of the audit table — allocate an ordinal, write an `issued_certificate` row, allocate again; assert the second ordinal is strictly greater AND does NOT equal `issued_certificate_rows().len()` after a row gap (pins 'not derived from len()' + gap semantics in one assertion). Wire the harness invocation from both adapters' existing test suites (sim: a default-lane acceptance test; store-local: an integration-tests-gated test, since it touches a real redb file). (6) `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/integration/` — add the host-only durable-across-reopen test (architecture.md §4.3 assertion 3): allocate, drop+reopen the `LocalObservationStore` on the same redb path, allocate again; assert strictly greater. Gated `integration-tests` per testing.md § 'Integration vs unit gating' (reopens a real redb file). Verify: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests` (Lima-routed on macOS) compiles green; `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core -p overdrive-sim -p overdrive-store-local -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` runs the conformance + reopen tests green; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core` green (new rustdoc); `cargo xtask dst-lint` green (overdrive-core addition is pure trait surface — IssuanceOrdinal is a newtype over u64, no Instant/tokio::net/rand). Single cohesive commit (no --no-verify — the suite is green at commit time). Commit trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01`.
criteria: Exactly the pinned additive method next_issuance_ordinal(&self) -> Result<IssuanceOrdinal, ObservationStoreError> lands on ObservationStore with the full architecture.md §3.1 rustdoc; no other trait signature changes; no new CaIssuanceError variant.

Host LocalObservationStore implements it via a single-key issuance_ordinal_counter redb table allocated atomically in one begin_write/commit on spawn_blocking; fresh store returns IssuanceOrdinal::new(0); table independent of ISSUED_CERTIFICATES_TABLE.

Sim SimObservationStore implements it via an in-memory Mutex<u64> incremented under parking_lot with no .await across the lock; fresh store returns 0; ca_issuance.rs swaps the len() block for next_issuance_ordinal and rewrites its Preconditions/Errors rustdoc.

run_issuance_ordinal_conformance sibling harness drives both adapters and asserts monotonic-and-unique-under-concurrency plus independence-from-the-audit-table; a host-only integration-tests-gated test asserts durable-across-reopen; no row/envelope/golden-bytes/append-only change; no migration code.

Lima-routed gates exit 0: cargo check --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests; cargo nextest run (conformance + reopen tests green, actually run not --no-run); cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core; cargo xtask dst-lint; clippy -D warnings. Single cohesive commit with Step-ID: 01-01 trailer.
acceptance_criteria: architecture.md §3.1 postconditions — `next_issuance_ordinal` returns a value strictly greater than every prior return for the store (including across host restart); the counter is durably advanced BEFORE Ok returns (crash immediately after return cannot re-issue n); NO issued_certificates row is read or written by the call (verified by the conformance harness assertion 2: the second ordinal is strictly greater AND does not equal issued_certificate_rows().len() after a deliberate row gap).

architecture.md §4.3 assertion 1 (monotonic-and-unique under concurrency) — `run_issuance_ordinal_conformance` drives N concurrent/interleaved allocations against one store and asserts the N returned ordinals are N distinct, strictly-increasing IssuanceOrdinals, for BOTH the host and sim adapters from one harness definition.

architecture.md §4.3 assertion 2 (independent of the audit table) — the harness allocates, writes an issued_certificate row, allocates again, and asserts the second ordinal is strictly greater than the first and NOT equal to the post-write `len()` — pinning both 'not derived from len()' and the gap semantics (#226 delete-survival property).

architecture.md §4.3 assertion 3 (host-only durable across reopen) — an integration-tests-gated host test allocates, drops + reopens LocalObservationStore on the same redb path, allocates again, and asserts strictly greater (the durable-across-OS-restart guarantee the host carries; sim is exempt — its durability domain is the sim-process lifetime).

architecture.md §3.1 fresh-store edge case (D3) — the first call on a never-before-allocated store returns IssuanceOrdinal::new(0) on both adapters (keeps the 'ordinals start at 0' consumer expectation and the V1 golden fixtures valid).

architecture.md §3.1 gap semantics (D4) — the counter advance is committed unconditionally on Ok; an allocated-but-unwritten ordinal is burned; no reclaim path exists. The harness's assertion 2 exercises a gap and confirms strict ordering survives it.

RCA root cause A (representable collision) DISSOLVED — the ordinal is sourced from an atomically-allocated durable counter, not a len() read; two concurrent callers cannot draw the same value. The check-then-act TOCTOU at ca_issuance.rs is removed entirely (development.md § 'Check-and-act must be atomic' — make the collision unrepresentable).

RCA root cause B (silent corruption) DISSOLVED as a side effect — with no collision there is nothing to detect; NO new CaIssuanceError::OrdinalCollision variant is added (D9 — the RCA Option-A detection shape is explicitly NOT this fix).

architecture.md §5 / D8 precondition changes — `issue_and_audit`'s rustdoc retires the single-writer precondition and decouples the append-only precondition from ordinal monotonicity; # Errors cites the allocation failure as a CaIssuanceError::Audit source.

Scope guardrails honored (D11/D12/D13, architecture.md §8): no row/envelope/golden-bytes/append-only-serial-guard change; no migration code (fresh store -> counter at 0; delete-the-redb-file upgrade path); no expansion into #36 (the contract is explicitly per-store/per-node); no GitHub issue created/edited/closed.

CLAUDE.md § 'Implement to the design' — exactly the one pinned method is added; no second method, no error variant, no row-field change, no Option<IssuanceOrdinal> sentinel, no row-without-ordinal type (the rejected B2 surface). A reviewer verifies the diff against the pinned signature, not just that tests pass.
Commit message carries the `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; landed as a single cohesive commit with normal pre-commit hooks (no --no-verify — the suite is green at commit time).
steps.tier: Tier 1 (DST conformance harness — pure-Rust async over the ObservationStore trait, default lane; driven against the sim adapter in the default lane and against the host adapter under the `integration-tests` feature because it touches a real redb file). The host-only durable-across-reopen test is `integration-tests`-gated per testing.md § 'Integration vs unit gating'.
quality_gates: Workspace compiles green (Lima-routed on macOS): `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests` exits 0.

Test suite green (Lima-routed; actually RUNS the fixtures, NOT --no-run — per user-memory `no_run_gate_misses_boot_regressions`): `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core -p overdrive-sim -p overdrive-store-local -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` exits 0; the new `run_issuance_ordinal_conformance` cases (both adapters) and the host durable-across-reopen test are green.
Doctests green: `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core` exits 0 (the new `next_issuance_ordinal` rustdoc contract compiles if it carries a fenced example; otherwise n/a).

dst-lint clean: `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 — the overdrive-core addition is pure trait surface (IssuanceOrdinal is a newtype over u64; no Instant, no tokio::net, no rand on the core compile path).

Clippy clean: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` exits 0 on the four touched crates. Any clippy finding surfaced during the step is fixed in-scope, not deferred (user-memory `fix_clippy_dont_defer`).

Mutation gate (post-GREEN, `run_in_background: true` per testing.md § Mutation testing exception; Lima-wrapped on macOS because --features integration-tests participates): `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-store-local --file crates/overdrive-store-local/src/observation_backend.rs` and the sim counterpart `--package overdrive-sim --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/observation_store.rs`; kill rate >=80%. The load-bearing mutation surface is the two adapter allocate bodies — the increment (`+ 1`), the fresh-store seed (0/1), and the read-before-write ordering must each be killed by a conformance assertion. The trait method body is a delegating signature with little to mutate. Do NOT run `git checkout -- crates/` after the pass (user-memory `no_git_checkout_after_mutants`; cargo-mutants restores the tree itself).

Surface discipline (CLAUDE.md § 'Implement to the design'): `git grep -n 'next_issuance_ordinal' crates/` shows exactly one trait declaration + two adapter impls + the ca_issuance call site + the conformance harness call sites — NO second method, NO new CaIssuanceError variant (`git grep -n 'OrdinalCollision' crates/` returns no matches), NO change to the IssuedCertificateRow struct literal or its rkyv envelope/golden fixtures (`git diff` touches none of `*issued_certificate_row*` or `tests/schema_evolution/*`).

Single-cut / no-migration discipline (D12): no code path reconstructs the counter from `issued_certificate_rows().len()`; `git grep -n 'issued_certificate_rows' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/ca_issuance.rs` returns no matches in the ordinal path (the len() read is GONE).
deliverables: Additive `ObservationStore::next_issuance_ordinal` trait method with the full behaviour-contract rustdoc (architecture.md §3.1).
Host adapter impl: single-key `issuance_ordinal_counter` redb table + atomic allocate in one begin_write/commit on spawn_blocking (LocalObservationStore).
Sim adapter impl: in-memory Mutex<u64> counter + atomic allocate under the existing parking_lot discipline (SimObservationStore).
Call-site swap in ca_issuance.rs::issue_and_audit (len() block -> next_issuance_ordinal) + the rewritten # Preconditions / # Errors rustdoc.
`run_issuance_ordinal_conformance` harness in overdrive-core::testing::observation_store, invoked from both adapter test suites.
steps.implementation_notes: PINNED SIGNATURE (architecture.md §3.1 / wave-decisions.md): `async fn next_issuance_ordinal(&self) -> Result<IssuanceOrdinal, ObservationStoreError>;` on `trait ObservationStore`. Returns the `overdrive_core::id::IssuanceOrdinal` newtype (D2 — NOT a bare u64; already imported in the trait module, no new import). This is THE contract. Per CLAUDE.md § 'Implement to the design — never invent API surface': build ONLY this one method. Do NOT add a new CaIssuanceError variant (D9), a second trait method, a row-field change, an Option<IssuanceOrdinal> sentinel, or a row-without-ordinal input type (those were the REJECTED B2 shape, architecture.md §2). If a primitive seems missing or the design under-specifies a shape, STOP and surface a blocker — do not reach for the nearest mechanism that compiles. FRESH-STORE = 0 (D3): the first call on a never-before-allocated store returns IssuanceOrdinal::new(0) on BOTH adapters (matches pre-fix len()-of-empty-table semantics; keeps the V1 golden fixtures and 'ordinals start at 0' consumer expectation valid). GAP SEMANTICS (D4): the counter advance commits unconditionally on Ok; an allocated-but-unwritten ordinal is BURNED (sequence may have holes) — this is correct because the consumer's max_by_key(issuance_ordinal) needs strict ORDERING, not density; no reclaim path. NO MIGRATION CODE (D12): fresh store -> 0; delete-the-redb-file is the upgrade path; do NOT reconstruct the counter from len() of existing rows (that re-creates the defect). NO ROW/ENVELOPE/GOLDEN-BYTES CHANGE (D11/architecture.md §8): the ordinal stays a caller-set field on the IssuedCertificateRow V1 payload; only its SOURCE changes; the rkyv schema-evolution surface and the append-only serial guard are untouched. HOST ATOMICITY (D5): one begin_write -> read-current -> insert(current+1 / initial) -> commit on the spawn_blocking pool (no blocking redb on a tokio worker thread, development.md § 'No blocking std::fs inside async fn'); redb serializable isolation linearises writers (same TOCTOU-safety as the existing apply_alloc_status_lww / apply_issued_certificate read-before-write). The counter table is independent of ISSUED_CERTIFICATES_TABLE (deleting audit rows does not rewind it — #226 delete-survival). SIM ATOMICITY (D6): in-memory Mutex<u64> initialized to 0, read-increment-return under the lock, no .await across the lock (development.md § 'Never hold a lock across .await'); deterministic under a seeded DST run (allocation follows deterministic call order -> bit-identical replay). CALL-SITE (architecture.md §3.2): `let ordinal = observation.next_issuance_ordinal().await.map_err(CaIssuanceError::audit)?;` — reuse CaIssuanceError::audit (allocation failure is an audit-path failure: issuance refuses, no unaudited cert escapes), row construction unchanged. CONFORMANCE HARNESS is the PRIMARY test deliverable (RCA §4 / architecture.md §4.3): a classic red-against-current-behaviour regression test is NOT constructible (the retired precondition IS 'no concurrent issuance', so a colliding test must violate the contract by construction); the harness instead pins the FIX's contract — monotonic-and-unique-under-concurrency (assertion 1, both adapters), independence-from-the-audit-table / not-derived-from-len + gap (assertion 2, both adapters), host-durable-across-reopen (assertion 3, host integration test only). TRAIT CONTRACT RUSTDOC per development.md § 'Trait definitions specify behavior': preconditions / postconditions / edge-cases / observable-invariants / errors VERBATIM from architecture.md §3.1 — the equivalence harness is the structural guard that both adapters honor it identically. dst-lint stays green (overdrive-core addition is pure trait surface). TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): the conformance harness MUST be written as property-based tests using proptest @-strategy generators where the contract supports it (development.md § 'Property-based testing') — generate N and an interleaving schedule, assert the returned set is |N| distinct strictly-increasing IssuanceOrdinals (the universe is the full set of returned ordinals; the invariant is total-order + cardinality). The independence-from-len assertion is a property over (number-of-allocations, number-of-rows-written, gap-positions). Single-example tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used (e.g. the host durable-across-reopen test is single-example by nature: it verifies wiring across a real redb reopen, not an invariant over generated inputs).
